summary: Provision a new transit gateway and attach VPCs to it. Greenfield today (no transit gateway exists yet in this estate) — the gateway create stands up an unattached hub; each attachment create joins one VPC to it, subject to the gateway's own route-table associations/propagations.

operations.title: Provision a new transit gateway
operations.summary: Request a brand-new transit gateway; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_ec2_transit_gateway as a complete specification: description, ASN and default route-table behavior. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. This provisions the transit gateway itself, with no VPCs attached yet.

params.name: tgw_name
params.label: Transit gateway name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the gateway in the generated draft — a transit gateway has no name field of its own.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: Shown in the console; distinct from the Name tag.

params.name: amazon_side_asn
params.label: Amazon-side ASN
params.help: Private 16-bit ASN for the Amazon side of any BGP sessions. Leave at the default unless on-prem needs a specific value.

params.name: default_route_table_association
params.label: Auto-associate new attachments
params.help: enable puts every new attachment into the default route table automatically — the common case for a simple hub-and-spoke.

params.name: default_route_table_propagation
params.label: Auto-propagate routes
params.help: enable propagates every new attachment's routes into the default route table automatically.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Transit gateway name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this gateway is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this gateway.

decisions: Auto-associate and auto-propagate default to enabled — a simple hub-and-spoke where every attachment shares one route table. Turn both off first if this gateway needs isolated route tables per attachment (network segmentation)
Multicast support, auto-accept for shared attachments, and additional transit gateway CIDR blocks are engineer decisions
VPC attachments are created as a separate follow-up step (the existing create-a-VPC-attachment action) — this transit gateway starts with nothing attached

operations.title: Provision a new transit gateway VPC attachment
operations.summary: Request a brand-new VPC attachment to an existing transit gateway; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_ec2_transit_gateway_vpc_attachment as a complete specification: transit gateway, VPC and subnets. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: attachment_key
params.label: Attachment identifier
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the attachment in the generated draft.

params.name: transit_gateway
params.label: Transit gateway
params.help: The existing transit gateway this VPC attaches to. Create a transit gateway first if the picker is empty.

params.name: subnets
params.label: Subnets
params.help: One subnet per Availability Zone the attachment should use — the transit gateway places an interface in each.

params.name: dns_support
params.label: DNS support
params.help: Resolves DNS hostnames across the transit gateway for this attachment.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Attachment name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this attachment is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this attachment.

decisions: Attaching a VPC to a transit gateway can expose it to every other VPC or on-prem network already attached, depending on the transit gateway's route table associations — confirm the target route table policy before attaching, especially on a shared hub
Appliance mode (for symmetric routing through a firewall/appliance VPC) and IPv6 support are engineer decisions
Transit gateway route table associations/propagations beyond the gateway's own default are engineer decisions (the transit gateway's own route table actions)
